top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 149, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Wadi Dayqah Challenge's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Wadi Dayqah Challenge's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before January.
Wadi Dayqah Challenge's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 149 miles.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 149 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
At Wadi Dayqah Challenge, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Wadi Dayqah Challenge. Train it until it feels boring.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
149 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Varied gradients on mixed terrain demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Wadi Dayqah Challenge's mixed terrain actually does to you.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 149 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.

race_overlay.heat: January in Muscat, Oman can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Wadi Dayqah Challenge. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Wadi Dayqah Challenge’s 149 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Wadi Dayqah Challenge. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Wadi Dayqah Challenge requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 149 miles of mixed terrain in Muscat, Oman.
